Dr. S. Iyer
MBBS, MD (General Medicine)  |  Consultant – Internal Medicine

Patient Details
Patient Name
Deepak Shah
Age / Sex
34 Y / Male
Patient ID
APL-2024-084312
Date of Consultation
23-May-2026
Visit Type
OPD – Outpatient
Insurance / Employee ID
EMP010 – TechCorp Solutions Pvt Ltd
Diagnosis / Clinical Impression
Acute Bronchitis  (ICD-10: J20.9 – Acute bronchitis, unspecified)
Medications Prescribed
S.No Drug Name & Strength Dosage Duration Instructions
1 Tab. Amoxicillin 500 mg
Penicillin-class antibiotic		 1 – 0 – 1
(BD)		 7 days After food. Complete full course.
2 Salbutamol Inhaler (100 mcg/puff)
Ventolin® / Asthalin®		 2 puffs PRN 10 days Shake well. Use spacer if available. Rinse mouth after use.
3 Syr. Bromhexine HCl 8 mg/5 mL
Mucolytic agent		 10 mL – 0 – 10 mL
(BD)		 5 days After meals. Do not crush or chew tablets.
4 Tab. Paracetamol 650 mg
Antipyretic / analgesic		 1 – 1 – 1
(TID)		 3 days / SOS For fever / body ache. Max 4 g/day.
Advice: Rest for 3–5 days. Adequate hydration (2–3 L/day). Steam inhalation twice daily. Avoid cold beverages and smoky environments. Return if fever persists beyond 3 days or breathing worsens. Wear a mask in public spaces.
Investigations Ordered
  • CBC with differential (to rule out bacterial superinfection)
  • CRP (C-Reactive Protein) – if fever persists after 72 hours
  • Chest X-ray PA view – only if no improvement by Day 4
Follow-up:   After 7 days or earlier if symptoms worsen.    OPD Slot booked: 30-May-2026, 10:30 AM – Dr. S. Iyer (Chamber 204)
